title: Static Accessibility Evidence, WCAG Mapping, and Review Handoff · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in WCAG-linked static finding ledger and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using surrounding purpose and review uncertainty.
Audit document language, headings, landmarks, labels, instructions, table relationships, and duplicate identifiers as evidence, not templates.
Identify native semantics and attribute risks while noting accessible names, roles, values, and states may need browser computation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Image Purpose
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 9259 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to map static image, structure, semantics, interaction risks, uncertainty, and WCAG review boundaries; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using surrounding purpose and review uncertainty.

Inspect the Image Purpose trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Image Purpose.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using surrounding purpose and review uncertainty. For Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Image Purpose, keep Image Purpose connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

For inspect the failure record, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Image Purpose, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Conformance Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-conformance-boundary-1d3gw3" that uses case 4588 to map static image, structure, semantics, interaction risks, uncertainty, and WCAG review boundaries. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Conformance Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Conformance Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Conformance Boundary, keep Conformance Boundary connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Link findings to WCAG 2.2 criteria, affected users, page state, severity rationale, evidence, uncertainty, and human owner without conformance claims.
